channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Splash pads associated with waterborne disease outbreaks: CDC
item.description: "Children are at increased risk for exposure to pathogens in contaminated splash pad water," the CDC said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, WGN 9

item.title: Hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO heads into third day as new clues emerge
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As the investigation into a masked gunman who stalked and killed the head of one of the largest U.S. health insurers moved into its third day Friday, possible leads emerged about his travel before the shooting and a message scrawled on ammunition found at the crime scene.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UnitedHealth Group says 'our hearts are broken' by murder of CEO Bryan Thompson
item.description: UnitedHealth Group and the company's many employees continue to grieve the loss of CEO Brian Thompson following the shooting that took his life outside the Hilton in Midtown Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yes, Chipotle is raising menu prices again: Here's why
item.description: The cost of your burrito bowl is going up. Chipotle has raised menu prices for the first time in over a year.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KREX 5

item.title: 186 minors cited in Utah bar sting
item.description: Over 180 citations were issued to minors for being inside a Sugar House bar following an investigation into concerns of "serious violations" of Utah's alcohol laws.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: We're all saying Daniel Craig's name wrong
item.description: His name may no longer be "Bond, James Bond," but former 007 Daniel Craig is setting the record straight about how to pronounce his name.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Father of victim in NYC subway chokehold case sues defendant Daniel Penny
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The father of the victim at the center of the fatal New York City subway chokehold trial has sued the defendant as a Manhattan jury continued to deliberate the case Thursday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police test DNA and fingerprints on discarded bottle as they hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO's killer
item.description: The masked gunman who killed the leader of one of the largest U.S. health insurance companies in front of midtown Manhattan surveillance cameras remained at large Thursday as the nation’s largest police department hunted for him.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Secret Service director touts changes as Congress presses him on Trump assassination attempt
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The acting director of the Secret Service said Thursday that the agency is “reorganizing and reimagining” its culture and how it operates following an assassination attempt against Donald Trump on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NASA pushes back astronaut flights to the moon again
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA announced more delays Thursday in sending astronauts back to the moon more than 50 years after Apollo. Administrator Bill Nelson said the next mission in the Artemis program -- flying four astronauts around the moon and back – is now targeted for April 2026.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Things to know about the federal investigation into the Memphis Police Department
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A U.S. Department of Justice investigation found that the Memphis Police Department committed a host of civil rights violations, including using excessive force, making illegal traffic stops and disproportionately targeting Black people in the majority Black city.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Link to adult website on Mattel's 'Wicked' dolls at center of proposed class action lawsuit
item.description: A mother claims she and her daughter visited the link on the box, only to be "horrified" by what they saw, according to the lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: You Are Right to Hate DC
item.description: Worth a click: Transgender rights activists went into the women’s bathroom at the Capitol in an act of “c**** civil disobedience” — their words, not mine. Watch the video here. Deck Chairs on the Titanic DOGE in the house: Elon Musk got a hero’s welcome on Capitol Hill today.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Secret Service leader, GOP rep get into shouting match over 9/11 questioning
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: At a sinkhole above an abandoned Pennsylvania coal mine, hopes of a rescue dwindle
item.description: Three days after a Pennsylvania woman searching for her cat disappeared at the chasm of a three-story deep sinkhole, the digging crews were back at it Thursday, hoping for a miracle but expecting to recover her remains.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dollar Tree could change prices, remove merchandise amid Trump's tariff plan
item.description: Dollar Tree announced they are ready to make certain changes amid President-elect Donald Trump's tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Madie MacDonald, Fox 5

item.title: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield reverses decision to cap anesthesia coverage after a set time limit
item.description: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield said in a statement that its decision to backpedal resulted from “significant widespread misinformation” about the policy.
item.dc:creator: Melanie Higgins, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Photos show suspected killer's face after shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: Police on Thursday shared new images of the suspected shooter in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US government is closing a women's prison and other facilities after years of abuse and decay
item.description: The Associated Press has learned that the federal Bureau of Prisons is permanently closing its “rape club” women’s prison in California and will idle six facilities in a sweeping realignment after years of abuse, decay and mismanagement.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Michael Balsamo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Illinois homeowner feels underground mine is splitting her house in two
item.description: About 330,000 Illinois homes sit on top of old abandoned mines. Some say their homes are sinking because of it.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO assassin left message behind to 'make a statement' or 'throw off police': Detectives
item.description: Words etched on bullet casings at the scene of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's murder were left behind "to make a statement," an NYPD detective said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Head of Democratic Caucus says thin House margins make attendance vital next year
item.description: The head of the House Democratic Caucus is sending an early message to his troops heading into the 119th Congress: Attendance will be crucial given the razor-tight margins in the lower chamber. “It’s important for every member to come to work and to do their job.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Waymo unveils plan to bring its robotaxi service to Miami
item.description: Waymo is gearing up to bring its robotaxi service to Miami which will accelerate an expansion that's been happening while its hobbled rivals remain in its rearview mirror.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Piles of mail dumped on Memphis street
item.description: The United States Postal Service said it was caused by a contractor that "inadvertently dropped some of its load.
item.dc:creator: Brian Didlake, NewsNation

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, ABC 4

item.title: Ammunition from UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting had words written on it: sources
item.description: Bullets found on the ground where UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson was shot Wednesday had writing on them, sources told Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Kala Rama, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects allegations
item.description: The offensive is among the deadliest and most destructive since World War II, and has destroyed vast areas of the besieged coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's illegal immigration crackdown welcomed by border town police strained by Biden policies
item.description: President-elect Trump's border policies, particularly his "Remain in Mexico" policy, could help police officers in border towns and Border Patrol agents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Associated Press’ top photos of 2024 from the US
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The right frame can freeze a moment in time, creating meaning for the masses from a fist pump over a bloodied ear, a bridge crumbled by a ship, towns shredded by nature, and a victory sealed with an on-field kiss. In 2024, photographers across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court leans toward upholding Tennessee's restrictions on gender-affirming care
item.description: The Supreme Court’s conservatives appeared to lean toward upholding Tennessee’s ban on gender-affirming care for minors in a blockbuster showdown over transgender rights Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: New clues emerge as investigators hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: New clues are emerging in the hunt for the masked gunman who stalked and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, including possible leads about his travel before the shooting and a message scrawled on ammunition found at the crime scene.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Karen Matthews And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Underground voids from abandoned mines pose danger to people and property when land collapses
item.description: Swaths of Pennsylvania and many other states are honeycombed with old, unstable mines that can cause the earth to suddenly give way and threaten people and property.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL RUBINKAM and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police say searchers don’t expect to find woman in Pennsylvania sinkhole alive
item.description: UNITY TOWNSHIP, Pa. (AP) — The search for a woman who is believed to have fallen into a sinkhole in western Pennsylvania has become a recovery effort after two treacherous days of digging through mud and rock produced no signs of life, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: GENE PUSKAR and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: Ohio school to offer new religious release program through Satanic Temple
item.description: A new religious release program for students is entering public schools in Marysville, Ohio, and this one emphasizes Satanic studies.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, KRON4

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi's family says search is 'far from over' after police reveal she voluntarily left for Mexico
item.description: Authorities may consider Hannah Kobayashi a "voluntary missing person," but her family isn't done looking for her.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he would block US Steel acquisition by Nippon Steel
item.description: The Japanese firm announced last year that it would acquire U.S. Steel Corp. in a $14.9 billion deal — a move that was sharply criticized by lawmakers on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5
